status_detail: Industrial SOEC electrolyzer demonstration at Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne. Part of the SLIND project (Siemens-Linde-Intermembrane-Debiotech). Genvia, the French SOEC joint venture between Schlumberger, CEA, Vicat, and others, is developing high-temperature steam electrolysis technology targeting 90%+ efficiency. This 1 MW SOEC demonstrator is among the first industrial-scale SOEC installations in France.

data_management.narrative_summary: Genvia is a French SOEC (Solid Oxide Electrolysis) venture developed by Schlumberger, CEA, Vicat, and other partners, targeting high-temperature steam electrolysis with over 90% electrical efficiency. The SLIND project at Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne is a 1 MW industrial SOEC demonstration, one of the first industrial-scale SOEC installations in France. SOEC technology operates at high temperatures (700-800C) and can utilize industrial waste heat, offering the highest electrical efficiency of any electrolysis technology. Genvia is targeting cost-competitive green hydrogen production for hard-to-abate industrial sectors including steel and ammonia. The project is supported by the French government and the Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes region.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected. Industrial waste heat integration potential from local industry.

offtake.offtaker_name: Local industrial users (ArcelorMittal potential offtake collaboration announced)

sources.title: Genvia — SOEC technology development and SLIND project announcements
